MODULE ONE
Building Your List

TRANSCRIPT
MODULE ONE – Building Your List
2
© 2017 Internet Alchemy, Inc. 	I 	www.ProductLaunchFormula.com
Executive Summary:
• 	Always be focused on your list and building your list – the wealth is in the list.
• 	No one starts out with a list – but successful people start building their list early and
often.
• 	Get the need for a big list out of your mind. Everyone starts with zero subscribers and
they build it from there.
• 	Small lists are strong. Your conversions from your first 1000 are going to be strong – I’ve
seen many time small lists outperform much bigger lists.
• 	To build your list you need three things: a squeeze page, a lead magnet (or opt-in bribe),
and traffic.
• 	Launches are a great way to build your list.
• 	Social media makes it easy to share your Prelaunch Content.
• 	Slow and steady wins the race.
• 	The actual list and the size of the list is not very important. It’s your relationship to the
list that’s important. (Do they open your emails, click links, respond?)
• 	If you put out good content, and you have a good Sideways Sales Letter, people are going
to love you. (They will not hate you for sending them Prelaunch Content.)
• 	Your list is the warmest right after the launch.
• 	Building a list is about baby steps.
• 	Leave a comment on this video and let me know, have you started your list? Or just say
“I’m here Jeff, and I’m ready!”
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com

TRANSCRIPT:
Okay, so this is about the absolute number one core strategy that I've used
over the years to make millions, and millions, and millions of dollars. Help my
students and clients make hundreds, and hundreds of millions of dollars, and that
number one core strategy no matter what product, or niche, business model that you
are in, is to always, always, always be focused on your list, and building
your list.
By list, I mean an email list. I don't mean your Twitter followers, or your
Facebook fans, or whatever. I'm talking about email. Email is and always has been
online, the absolute king. Yes, there could be a time in the future somewhere where
email isn't king, but it is the king right now. It still has been ... I've seen it over, and
over, and over.
I've coached so many people. In launches and otherwise. When you post
something to social, you might get a few friends sharing it, you might get a few
people commenting on it.
When you push the button to send an email, it drives action, it drives clicks.
It's just breathtaking when I can hit the send button and literally send thousands of
people, within minutes, to a website. It's just crazy. I mean that's the scale I'm
playing it right now.
It doesn't matter. If you have a 100 people on your email list. That's still 100
people who have invited you into their inboxes, which is a very private place. With a
100 people, you can create some serious impact.
Your number one rule is to always be focused on your list and
building your list.
The wealth is in the list. Your business is in the list. Your impact is in list.
Now, when I talk about lists, one thing first of all, I am so passionate about this that I
give you a complete course launching your list within product launch formula.
That course is all about building a list and profiting from that list. This video
here is a bit of an overview and I'm going to go deep in launching your list. When we
talk about lists, there's lots of ways to split it up to talk about it.

© 2017 Internet Alchemy, Inc. 	I 	www.ProductLaunchFormula.com
One core, fundamentally you have two lists. You have a list of prospects,
and a list of clients, or customers.
The difference is prospects haven't bought anything from you yet and
customers have, clients have. You'll want to treat those two lists differently,
it's a fundamentally different conversation you have with those two lists. Oh, by the
way, the list of clients and customers, is way, way more powerful. 10 to 15 times more
powerful. A list of clients that is a 1000 names on it, will be as powerful as a list of
prospects that is 10 or 12 or 15,000 names on it.
That's just a little preview of what's in Launching Your List.
Now, early on I stumbled onto to this whole idea of building a list and I've
always focused on it. I think I started actually building a list way back in the day, in
the 90s when I started out. It was really hard to build five pages and it was sort of
rocket science for me. It was really easy to send an email and I just built my list
initially by blind carbon copy, I would send out my emails. That was before I had a
list host, or could afford a list host, or before list hosts really existed the way they do
now.
In any case, I started building that list before I ever put up a website, put up a
web page. Then, I could see the power and control it gave me when I sent that email,
I would get, you know within minutes, within seconds, I would start to get a reaction.
That's the thing about email and an email list, is it gives you power, and
control. If you build a following on YouTube, or Facebook, or Snapchat, or whatever,
that platform could go away. They could change the rules of that platform and you
don't have any control over that platform. When you have an email list, you have
control. You own that list. If one list provider stopped sending your emails out, you
can move to a different list provider. You've got the list. You have power and you
have control.
At the end of the day, there are not that many things you can count on in
business, but a list you can count on. You just have to really develop an obsession
with list building.
I'll tell you what, lead acquisition – you measure how well you are doing at
getting people to join your list. Then how your relationship with that list, which can
be measured in how people respond to your emails. In the clicks, and the opens, and
whether they follow what they tell you to do in the email. Those are the most

important metrics that I will look at in any business, when I'm looking at the
business.
Now, a key here is ... we see a lot of people, they want to have a huge list. Even
worse, they're like ... you know if I start building a list, it's not going to do me any
good until I have at least, you know, ten or fifteen thousand people, or I've got a
1,000 people, or I've got 5,000, or I wish I had 30,000. It's not going to do me any
good until I have that big list. Nothing could be further from the truth, nothing. You
know, nothing could be further from the truth.
With just a 100 people on your list, you can do amazing things. With a 1000
people on your list, you can do an amazing thing. You don't need a huge list. No one
starts with a huge list. I didn't start with a huge list. I didn't magically come out of the
womb with an email last. I built my email list, and I built it by getting my first
subscriber then moving on from there.
The thing is, small lists can be really powerful. In fact, on a per name
basis, small lists are always more powerful. It's just sort of like this universal law. On
a per person basis, a 1000 person list is going to be stronger than 100,000 person
list. I've seen this over and over.
I'm on the inside of so many launches. I get to see the inner workings of
launches, and when you have a joint venture launch where other people are mailing
for you. They're mailing it to your opt-in page, so you can start to see how powerful
other people's lists are. You can see how much traffic they send, you can see how they
opt-in, you can see how they end up converting. I've seen many times where a
10,000-person list will outperform a 100,000 list. It comes down to
relationships. There is real power in small lists. Even with small lists you can ... I
mean, I'm talking a 100-person, 200-person, 300-person lists, can be really, really
strong.
Now to build your list you just need three things.
1. You need a squeeze page, or an opt-in page.
2. You need a lead magnet, that's something that people are
going to get after they opt-in. That's sort of this thing you
dangle in front of them to get to them to say, "Yes, I want to
give you my email address."

3. Then you just have to drive traffic to the squeeze page.
I'll go deeper into this when launching your list. All of this is way easier than
it used to be.
A squeeze page used to be ... your opt-in page used to be really hard. Now,
there's tools that let you build it really quickly and easily. Lead magnets, a lot easier
than they used to be. It could be video, it could audio, it could be a PDF. All those
things are easier to create than they used to be.
Traffic is easier than it used to be, both organic traffic, say like from social
media or from forums. Also, paid traffic from Facebook.
Of course, underscoring all this, launches are an amazing way to build
to your list. I've literally added tens of thousands of people to my lists within, you
know, within days. I had a launch where we headed almost, I think it was almost
90,000 people over a 10-day period. It's absolutely amazing. Now, I've got a great JV
partner, joint venture partners and that's why I was able to do that.
Even in a seed launch or an internal launch, if you've put out great Prelaunch
Content, you'll start to build your list organically through word of mouth. The
difference there is, it's Prelaunch Content that follows that sideways sales ... there’s a
structure. That opportunity transformation ownership structure is so compelling
versus this content marketing system term out there now and people do all this
content marketing.
Content for content's sake, doesn't do anything ... there's too much
content out there, but when it's in that form, the sideways sales letter. That gets
people excited, that gets your content shared, and that's how you can build your list.
Slow and steady wins the race.
When I started out way back, I was getting one subscriber every four days and
I worked really hard. I got up to one a day and then I worked really hard and got up
to four a day. I kept on working, and working, and working, and eventually get that
up to 30 a day. The thing about 30 a day, that means 900 a month. 900 a month,
that's like 10,000 people a year. That was one newsletter I was publishing and that
newsletter got 30 a day.
I eventually got up to 80,000 people on that list and that was pretty freaking

awesome. It went from 30 a day, there was some other things that I did, but still it's

Again, the size of the list is not nearly as important as your relationship with
the list. Do they open your emails, do they click, do they respond? Always
relationship trump list size.
Now, if you put out great content and you've put out a good sideways sales
letter. People are going to love you. They're not going to hate you for sending
Prelaunch Content. I see this over and over. People come out of a launch with the
best lists they've ever had. Both the people that buy from you, they've been through
the sideways sales letter, they love you. Even the prospects, the people who didn't
buy from you in the launch, they'll end up loving you. I've seen this over and over
where someone comes out of a launch. They have the most powerful lists in terms of
open rates, and click rates, and responsiveness.
Through all this, the number one thing you need to remember is building a
list is all about baby steps. It's about those three basics I told you about. You
getting that opt-in page up, putting together a great lead magnet for them, so they do
want to join you. Then starting to drive traffic to it. It's just about baby steps
and getting your first subscriber.
